parameters.help: Standard deviation of Gaussian noise to add to backbone atoms
Batch size; can set higher for titan, quadro GPUs, reduce this if running out of GPU memory
Path to a dictionary which specifies AA composion bias if neededi, e.g. {A: -1.1, F: 0.7} would make A less likely and F more likely.
Path to dictionary with per position bias.
Parse CA-only structures and use CA-only models (default: false)
Path to a dictionary specifying which chains need to be designed and which ones are fixed, if not specied all chains will be designed.
0 for False, 1 for True; output conditional probabilities p(s_i given the rest of the sequence and backbone)
0 for False, 1 for True; if true output conditional probabilities p(s_i given backbone)
Path to a dictionary with fixed positions
Path to a folder with parsed pdb into jsonl
Max sequence length
ProteinMPNN model name: v_48_002, v_48_010, v_48_020, v_48_030; v_48_010=version with 48 edges 0.10A noise
Number of sequences to generate per target
Path to a dictionary which specifies which amino acids need to be omited from design at specific chain indices
Specify which amino acids should be omitted in the generated sequence, e.g. 'AC' would omit alanine and cystine.
Path to a folder to output sequences, e.g. /home/out/
score provided input sequence in a fasta format; e.g. GGGGGG/PPPPS/WWW for chains A, B, C sorted alphabetically and separated by /
Path to model weights folder;
Path to a single PDB to be designed
Define which chains need to be designed for a single PDB
0 for False, 1 for True
Path to a dictionary with pssm
0 for False, 1 for True
A value between [0.0, 1.0], 0.0 means do not use pssm, 1.0 ignore MPNN predictions
A value between -inf + inf to restric per position AAs
A string of temperatures, 0.2 0.25 0.5. Sampling temperature for amino acids. Suggested values 0.1, 0.15, 0.2, 0.25, 0.3. Higher values will lead to more diversity.
0 for False, 1 for True; save MPNN predicted probabilites per position
0 for False, 1 for True; save score=-log_prob to npy files
0 for False, 1 for True; score input backbone-sequence pairs
If set to 0 then a random seed will be picked;
0 for False, 1 for True
Path to a dictionary with tied positions
0 for False, 1 for True; output unconditional probabilities p(s_i given backbone) in one forward pass
Flag to load ProteinMPNN weights trained on soluble proteins only.
Run multiple JAX model evaluations to obtain a timing that excludes the compilation time, which should be more indicative of the time required for inferencing many proteins.
Path to the BFD database for use by HHblits.
Path to directory of supporting data.
Choose preset MSA database configuration - smaller genetic database config (reduced_dbs) or full genetic database config  (full_dbs)

Paths to FASTA files, each containing a prediction target that will be folded one after another. If a FASTA file contains multiple sequences, then it will be folded as a multimer. Paths should be separated by commas. All FASTA paths must have a unique basename as the basename is used to name the output directories for each prediction.
Path to the HHblits executable.
Path to the HHsearch executable.
Number of CPUs to use for HHsearch. Defaults to min(cpu_count, 8). Going above 8 CPUs provides very little additional speedup.
Path to the hmmbuild executable.
Path to the hmmsearch executable.
Number of CPUs to use for HMMsearch. Defaults to min(cpu_count, 8). Going above 8 CPUs provides very little additional speedup.
Path to the JackHMMER executable.
Number of CPUs to use for Jackhmmer. Defaults to min(cpu_count, 8). Going above 8 CPUs provides very little additional speedup.
Path to the Kalign executable.
Maximum template release date to consider. Important if folding historical test sets.
Path to the MGnify database for use by JackHMMER.
Choose preset model configuration - the monomer model, the monomer model with extra ensembling, monomer model with pTM head, or multimer model

The models to run the final relaxation step on. If `all`, all models are relaxed, which may be time consuming. If `best`, only the most confident model is relaxed. If `none`, relaxation is not run. Turning off relaxation might result in predictions with distracting stereochemical violations but might help in case you are having issues with the relaxation stage.

How many predictions (each with a different random seed) will be generated per model. E.g. if this is 2 and there are 5 models then there will be 10 predictions per input. Note: this FLAG only applies if model_preset=multimer
Path to file containing a mapping from obsolete PDB IDs to the PDB IDs of their replacements.
Path to a directory that will store the results.
Path to the PDB70 database for use by HHsearch.
Full filepath to the PDB seqres database file (not just the directory) for use by hmmsearch.

The random seed for the data pipeline. By default, this is randomly generated. Note that even if this is set, Alphafold may still not be deterministic, because processes like GPU inference are nondeterministic.
Path to the small version of BFD used with the "reduced_dbs" preset.
Path to a directory with template mmCIF structures, each named <pdb_id>.cif
Path to the Uniprot database for use by JackHMMer.
Path to the UniRef30 database for use by HHblits.
Path to the Uniref90 database for use by JackHMMER.
Whether to relax on GPU. Relax on GPU can be much faster than CPU, so it is recommended to enable if possible. GPUs must be available if this setting is enabled.

Whether to read MSAs that have been written to disk instead of running the MSA tools. The MSA files are looked up in the output directory, so it must stay the same between multiple runs that are to reuse the MSAs. WARNING: This will not check if the sequence, database or configuration have changed.
Strictly increasing order of token sizes for which to cache compilations. For any input with more tokens than the largest bucket size, a new bucket is created for exactly that number of tokens.
If True, compresses the output mmCIF and confidences JSON files (the two largest files) using zstandard. Note that embeddings and distogram, if saved, are already stored in a compressed format.
Optional override for maximum number of iterations to run for RDKit conformer search.
Path to the directory containing the databases. Can be specified multiple times to search multiple directories in order.

AlphaFold 3 model training and evaluation filtered out leaving atoms from glycan ligands even if they were not bonded to anything ("standalone" glycans). Setting this flag to True fixes this undesirable behavior, but moves away from the regime where AlphaFold 3 was trained and evaluated.

Whether to force the output directory to be used even if it already exists and is non-empty. Useful to set this to True to run the data pipeline and the inference separately, but use the same output directory.

Optional override for the GPU device to use for inference, uses zero-based indexing. Defaults to the 0th GPU on the system. Useful on multi-GPU systems to pin each run to a specific GPU. Note that if GPUs are already pre-filtered by the environment (e.g. by using CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES), this flag refers to the GPU index after the filtering has been done.
Path to the Hmmalign binary.
Path to the Hmmbuild binary.
Path to the Hmmsearch binary.
Path to the directory containing input JSON files.
Path to the Jackhmmer binary.
Maximum number of shards to search against in parallel. If unset, one Jackhmmer instance will be run per shard. Only applicable if the database is sharded.
Number of CPUs to use for Jackhmmer. Defaults to min(cpu_count, 8). Going above 8 CPUs provides very little additional speedup.
Path to a directory for the JAX compilation cache.
Path to the input JSON file.

Maximum template release date to consider. Format: YYYY-MM-DD. All templates released after this date will be ignored. Controls also whether to allow use of model coordinates for a chemical component from the CCD if RDKit conformer generation fails and the component does not have ideal coordinates set. Only for components that have been released before this date the model coordinates can be used as a fallback.
Mgnify database path, used for protein MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in number of sequences for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
Path to the model to use for inference.
Path to the Nhmmer binary.
Maximum number of shards to search against in parallel. If unset, one Nhmmer instance will be run per shard. Only applicable if the database is sharded.
Number of CPUs to use for Nhmmer. Defaults to min(cpu_count, 8). Going above 8 CPUs provides very little additional speedup.
NT-RNA database path, used for RNA MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in megabases for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
Number of diffusion samples to generate.
Number of recycles to use during inference.

Number of seeds to use for inference. If set, only a single seed must be provided in the input JSON. AlphaFold 3 will then generate random seeds in sequence, starting from the single seed specified in the input JSON. The full input JSON produced by AlphaFold 3 will include the generated random seeds. If not set, AlphaFold 3 will use the seeds as provided in the input JSON.
Path to a directory where the results will be saved.
PDB database directory with mmCIF files path, used for template search.

Whether to deduplicate unpaired MSA against paired MSA. The default behaviour matches the method described in the AlphaFold 3 paper. Set this to false if providing custom paired MSA using the unpaired MSA field to keep it exactly as is as deduplication against the paired MSA could break the manually crafted pairing between MSA sequences.
Rfam database path, used for RNA MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in megabases for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
RNAcentral database path, used for RNA MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in megabases for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
Whether to run the data pipeline on the fold inputs.
Whether to run inference on the fold inputs.
Whether to save the final distogram in the output. Note that the distogram is a large float16 array: num_tokens * num_tokens * 64.
Whether to save the final trunk single and pair embeddings in the output. Note that the embeddings are large float16 arrays: num_tokens * 384 + num_tokens * num_tokens * 128.
PDB sequence database path, used for template search.
Small BFD database path, used for protein MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in number of sequences for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
UniProt database path, used for protein paired MSA search.
The Z-value representing the database size in number of sequences for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
UniRef90 database path, used for MSA search. The MSA obtained by searching it is used to construct the profile for template search.
The Z-value representing the database size in number of sequences for E-value calculation. Must be set for sharded databases.
The accelerator to use for prediction. Default is gpu.
An optional checkpoint, will use the provided Boltz-1 model by default.
Whether to add the Molecular Weight correction to the affinity value head.
Custom header key for API key authentication (default: X-API-Key).
Custom header value for API key authentication.
The directory where to download the data and model. Default is ~/.boltz, or $BOLTZ_CACHE if set.
An optional checkpoint, will use the provided Boltz-1 model by default.
The number of devices to use for prediction. Default is 1.
The number of diffusion samples to use for prediction. Default is 1.
The number of diffusion samples to use for affinity prediction. Default is 5.
YAML input file or directory.
The maximum number of MSA sequences to use for prediction. Default is 8192.
The maximum number of samples to predict in parallel. Default is None.
The method to use for prediction. Default is None.
The model to use for prediction. Default is boltz2.
Pairing strategy to use. Used only if --use_msa_server is set. Options are 'greedy' and 'complete'
MSA server password for basic auth. Used only if --use_msa_server is set. Can also be set via BOLTZ_MSA_PASSWORD environment variable.
MSA server url. Used only if --use_msa_server is set.
MSA server username for basic auth. Used only if --use_msa_server is set. Can also be set via BOLTZ_MSA_USERNAME environment variable.
Whether to disable the kernels. Default False
The number of MSA sequences to subsample. Default is 1024.
The number of dataloader workers to use for prediction. Default is 2.
The path where to save the predictions.
The output format to use for the predictions. Default is mmcif.
Whether to override existing found predictions. Default is False.
The number of threads to use for preprocessing. Default is 1.
The number of recycling steps to use for prediction. Default is 3.
The number of sampling steps to use for prediction. Default is 200.
The number of sampling steps to use for affinity prediction. Default is 200.
Seed to use for random number generator. Default is None (no seeding).

The step size is related to the temperature at which the diffusion process samples the distribution. The lower the higher the diversity among samples (recommended between 1 and 2). Default is 1.638 for Boltz-1 and 1.5 for Boltz-2. If not provided, the default step size will be used.
Whether to subsample the MSA. Default is True.
Whether to use the MMSeqs2 server for MSA generation. Default is False.
Whether to use potentials for steering. Default is False.
to dump the s and z embeddings into a npz file. Default is False.
Whether to dump the pae into a npz file. Default is True.
Whether to dump the pde into a npz file. Default is False.
